reforms.description: The Italian government has implemented several reforms to enhance the startup ecosystem, including the establishment of 'one-stop shops' to simplify regulatory procedures for businesses and the creation of the Department for Development and Cohesion Policies (DPS) to foster private investment in the Mezzogiorno. Additionally, an ambitious liberalisation programme for local public utilities aims to introduce market principles in essential services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to simplify business operations and encourage investment, which are expected to enhance entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at improving the business environment and fostering entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The mandate to unify these general principles into an operational framework comprising finely balanced rules and incentives was assigned to a newly formed branch of the Ministry of Treasury, the Department for Development and Cohesion Policies (DPS), which became operational in 1998.

reforms.description: Italy has established the Fondo di Ricerca Applicata (FRA) to support applied research, particularly for small and medium-sized enterprises, and has reformed the Fondo Innovazione Tecnologica (FIT) to streamline application procedures and provide tax credits for companies hiring researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These funding initiatives are designed to enhance research capabilities and incentivize innovation, which are crucial for economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: Both funding mechanisms are operational and supporting research and innovation activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are narrower in scope compared to major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: A number of actions have been taken to make FRA funds more accessible to small and medium-sized companies.

reforms.description: The Italian government has introduced a reform programme aimed at creating a broader and more equitable system of welfare spending, utilizing savings from pension reform to reduce contribution rates and enhance private pension roles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact on growth is ambiguous as it depends on the effectiveness of the welfare reforms in stimulating economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in welfare policy.
reforms.source_quote: The Italian authorities have recently launched a reform programme whereby savings from pension reform would be used to create a broader and more equitable system of welfare spending.
